subject: [swap tier discussion] Re: [PATCH v3 2/4] mm/zswap: Implement proactive writeback

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to Yosry's feedback that setting zswap as its own tier is meaningless without vswap, explaining that the patch needs a phys swap device for the backing slot allocation.
reviews.raw_body:
Without vswap, if you set zswap as its own tier, which phys swap
device should we allocate from for the backing slot? :)

With vswap then it makes sense (and would probably be the "default"
for zswap setup until we enable zswap writeback).

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that without vswap, setting zswap as its own tier is meaningless and suggested making it a no-op with a warning to users; also pointed out that memory.zswap.writeback is still needed until the non-vswap case for zswap is removed
reviews.raw_body:
Today we just allocate a swap slot in a swapfile during reclaim,
before swapout, and zswap will just writeback to that one. I assume
the same will work with swap tiering, except that maybe the way that
swap slot will respect the allowed swap tiers?

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged Yosry Ahmed's concern about setting zswap as its own tier, and explained that it would prevent allocation of a swap slot in a swapfile.
reviews.raw_body:
Yep! So if we set zswap as the only tier, then it wouldn't be able to
allocate a swap slot in swapfile right?

Or are you suggesting that if we set zswap as the only tier then we
can allocate from any swapfile (since we're not doing any IO anyway)?

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary:
Reviewer pointed out that allowing zswap as its own tier without vswap is meaningless and suggested making it a no-op, instead proposing to keep memory.zswap.writeback until the non-vswap case for zswap is removed.

Reviewer noted that having zswap as its own tier is equivalent to disabling writeback, but also pointed out that this would prevent zswap from being used because it cannot access swap slots without vswap enabled.
reviews.raw_body:
Ohh I thought you meant we shouldn't allow zswap to be a tier at all,
not the *only* tier.

---

Hmm, technically having zswap as the only tier should be equivalent to
disabling writeback, but you're right that if zswap is the only tier
than the memcg is not allowed to use swap slots from any swapfile, so
zswap cannot be used. Very good point :)

In this case I think yes, we need vswap to be enabled to allow making
zswap the only tier. That's one gap between zswap being the only tier
and disabling zswap writeback, the former requires vswap while the
latter doesn't.

2026-06-16.patch_summary: This patch prevents setting zswap as its own tier when vswap is not enabled, as it would be meaningless and potentially cause issues. Instead, it proposes to keep the memory.zswap.writeback interface until non-vswap cases for zswap are eliminated.
